fact.fact: The National Energy Regulator of South Africa (NERSA) was established in terms of Section 3 of the National Energy Regulator Act, 2004 (Act No. 40 of 2004).
fact.source_text: The National Energy Regulator of South Africa (NERSA) is a regulatory authority established as a juristic person in terms of Section 3 of the National Energy Regulator Act, 2004 (Act No. 40 of 2004).

verdict.reason: The source text directly and unambiguously states the fact.

fact.fact: NERSA regulates the electricity, piped-gas and petroleum pipelines industries in terms of the Electricity Regulation Act, 2006 (Act No. 4 of 2006), Gas Act, 2001 (Act No. 48 of 2001) and Petroleum Pipelines Act, 2003 (Act No. 60 of 2003).
fact.source_text: NERSA’s mandate is to regulate the electricity, piped-gas and petroleum pipelines industries in terms of the Electricity Regulation Act, 2006 (Act No. 4 of 2006), Gas Act, 2001 (Act No. 48 of 2001) and Petroleum Pipelines Act, 2003 (Act No. 60 of 2003).

verdict.reason: The source text directly and unambiguously states the fact.

fact.fact: The access to electricity in South Africa was at a lower percentage in 1994.
fact.source_text: Access to electricity in 1994 was at lower percentage.

verdict.reason: The source text directly states that access to electricity in 1994 was at a lower percentage, which matches the candidate fact.

fact.fact: NERSA is governed by nine members: five part-time and four full-time, including the Chief Executive Office (CEO).
fact.source_text: The structure of the Energy Regulator consists of nine members, five of whom are part-time and four are full-time, including the Chief Executive Office (CEO).

verdict.reason: The source text uses 'Chief Executive Office (CEO)' instead of 'Chief Executive Officer (CEO)'

fact.fact: Since 1994, the Department of Energy, through INEP, has electrified 7.2 million households using grid technology.

verdict.reason: The source text uses 'make it possible to electrify' which is a different verb than 'has electrified'.

fact.fact: As of the Community Survey in 2016, 90.3% of households in South Africa had access to electricity for lighting.

synthesis.content: The National Energy Regulator of South Africa (NERSA) regulates the electricity, piped-gas, and petroleum pipelines industries. NERSA was established in terms of the National Energy Regulator Act. The access to electricity in South Africa has improved from a lower percentage in the past.
